title: File Exchange and Report Distribution
body_text:
File Exchange and Report Distribution
1	Introduction to File Exchange and Report Distribution
1.1	Overview of File Exchange and Report Distribution
This Part describes how to upload and download files via Central 1芒聙聶s File and Report Exchange. The files may be data files or report files that include, but aren芒聙聶t limited to, the following products and services:
AFT
Clearing and deposit items
Bill payments
e-Transfers (ETFR/EMT)
Me-to-Me Transfers (M2M)
Central 1 account activity and statements
Your financial institution can connect to Central 1芒聙聶s File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
A direct connection (SFTP) is required if scripts are used to exchange files. Central 1 doesn芒聙聶t provide support for scripts or the FTP software used to connect to the File and Report Exchange, but it does support the connection. Contact the FTP software manufacturer for FTP software related questions.
Individuals who require access to the File and Report Exchange must log in via the Client Centre. Central 1 supports the connection and the user interface. Online access via the Client Centre isn芒聙聶t compatible with automated scripting processes.
It芒聙聶s a good practice to download files daily. However, files are available on Central 1芒聙聶s File and Report Exchange for 30 calendar days. The reports from the CRF* and ICR1 files will be purged after seven calendar days since these are advance versions of existing Canadian AFT clearing reports. You are responsible for downloading files to a separate location before the files are removed from the File and Report Exchange.
Report files are available in a text format (e.g., in the MERG file) and, if applicable, in Portable Document Format (PDF). If applicable, PDFs are located in the relevant subfolder by product even if the text file is in the root directory (main folder).
1.2	Enrolling Your Financial Institution in the File and Report Exchange
Contact Central 1 to enroll your organization in Central 1芒聙聶s File and Report Exchange. See Section 1.4, Contacts and Websites.
1.3	Providing File and Report Exchange Procedures to Business Members
If your financial institution芒聙聶s business members require access to Central 1芒聙聶s File and Report Exchange (e.g., to download files for Bill Payment Remittance Processing services or upload AFT files), you can distribute the applicable File and Report Exchange procedures:
Accessing the File and Report Exchange Online via the Client Centre 芒聙聯 Business Member Procedures, Form 3430
Accessing the File and Report Exchange via Direct Connection (SFTP) 芒聙聯 Business Member Procedures, Form 3431
